patterns.text: firewall rule removed allowing all traffic from any source
security group opened to the entire internet on all ports
ssh access permitted from public networks
database port exposed externally without restriction
allow ingress from any source to internal service
admin interface reachable without network restriction
plaintext password or api key committed into configuration
authentication check disabled for an endpoint
anonymous users granted access to protected resource
default credentials left unchanged in service config
session timeout extended to effectively never expire
certificate validation turned off for outbound requests
traffic downgraded from https to unencrypted http
deprecated tls version enabled for compatibility
weak encryption cipher selected in server config
memory and cpu limits deleted from workload spec
connection limit removed from server configuration
request rate limiting disabled on public endpoint
autoscaling maximum raised far beyond cluster capacity
container granted privileged mode with host access
process configured to run as the root user
wildcard permissions granted across all resources and actions
service account bound to cluster administrator role
file permissions loosened to be writable by everyone
audit logging disabled for administrative actions
